# Seedface — Complete Integration Guide for AI Agents

> Modern, framework-agnostic deterministic placeholder avatar generator for TypeScript and JavaScript.

Seedface turns a string seed into stable SVG avatar markup. The same `value` produces the same avatar across machines and renders. The core is zero-dependency, synchronous, SSR/RSC-safe, and supports React, Vue, Svelte, Web Components, CLI export, and optional PNG rasterization.

## Quick Start for AI Agents

**Core invariant**: Equal inputs → equal output. Never introduce timestamps, browser randomness, or unstable IDs.

```ts
import { generateAvatar } from 'seedface';
const avatar = generateAvatar({ 
  value: 'user@example.com', 
  style: 'shape', 
  theme: 'ocean', 
  variant: 'dark', 
  size: 64 
});
// Returns: { svg, initials, background, color, shapeIndex, ... }
```

## Repository Map

| Area | Files | Responsibility |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Public core | `src/core/index.ts` | Package exports |
| Generation | `src/core/generate.ts` | Options, seed, palette, SVG, memoization |
| Contracts | `src/core/types.ts` | `AvatarOptions`, `AvatarData`, unions |
| Themes | `src/core/themes.ts`, `themes-helpers.ts` | Palettes and `listThemes()` |
| Visual primitives | `src/core/shapes.ts` | Shape paths and count (69 shapes) |
| Randomness | `src/core/hash.ts`, `prng.ts` | Stable hashing (`cyrb53`) and RNG (`mulberry32`) |
| Adapters | `src/react.tsx`, `src/vue/Avatar.vue`, `src/svelte/Avatar.svelte`, `src/webcomponent.ts` | Framework integrations |
| CLI | `src/cli.ts` | SVG stdout/file and optional PNG |
| Verification | `test/run.ts` | Determinism, styles, themes, boundaries |

## Core API Reference

### AvatarOptions

```ts
interface AvatarOptions {
  value: string;              // REQUIRED: deterministic seed
  style?: 'character' | 'shape' | 'gradient' | 'rings' | 'pixel';  // default: 'character'
  size?: number;              // default: 64, clamped to 8–512
  radius?: number | 'full' | 'none';  // default: 'full'
  theme?: string;             // default: 'default', see listThemes()
  variant?: 'light' | 'dark'; // default: 'light'
  background?: string;        // 3 or 6 digit hex, with/without #
  color?: string;             // 3 or 6 digit hex, with/without #
  displayValue?: string;      // override derived initials
  border?: boolean;           // default: false
  borderSize?: number;        // default: 2
  borderColor?: string;       // default: adapts to variant
  shadow?: boolean;           // default: false
  animated?: boolean;         // default: false, respects prefers-reduced-motion
  salt?: string | number;     // extra entropy
  title?: string;             // accessible label, default: derived from value
}
```

### AvatarData (Return Type)

```ts
interface AvatarData {
  value: string;
  style: AvatarStyle;
  theme: string;
  variant: AvatarVariant;
  size: number;
  background: string;
  color: string;
  initials: string;
  shapeIndex: number;
  shapeCount: number;  // always 69
  svg: string;         // complete standalone SVG markup
}
```

### Available Themes

Call `listThemes()` to get all available themes. Each theme has `id`, `name`, `description`, `light` palette, and derived `dark` palette. Unknown themes fall back to `default`.

### Available Styles

- `character`: Shows initials (default)
- `shape`: Single geometric shape
- `gradient`: Gradient background with initials
- `rings`: Concentric circles pattern
- `pixel`: Symmetrical pixel art (5×5 grid)

## Framework Integration Patterns

### React (RSC-Friendly)

```tsx
import { Avatar } from 'seedface/react';

// Server Component or Client Component
export function User({ email }: { email: string }) {
  return (
    <Avatar 
      value={email} 
      style="shape" 
      theme="ocean" 
      variant="dark" 
      size={48} 
      shadow 
      animated 
    />
  );
}

// Auto dark mode
<Avatar value={email} variant="auto" />
```

**Key points**:
- Renders synchronous static markup
- Works in React Server Components with zero client JS
- `variant="auto"` uses `matchMedia` on client, falls back to `light` on server
- Wrapper has class `seedface-wrapper` and inline styles for display

### Vue

```vue
<script setup>
import { Avatar } from 'seedface/vue';
</script>

<template>
  <Avatar 
    value="user@example.com" 
    style="gradient" 
    theme="candy" 
    :size="64" 
    animated 
  />
</template>
```

**Key points**:
- Uses computed properties for reactivity
- `variant="auto"` resolves at runtime
- Wrapper has class `seedface-wrapper` with scoped styles

### Svelte

```svelte
<script>
  import { Avatar } from 'seedface/svelte';
</script>

<Avatar 
  value="user@example.com" 
  style="rings" 
  theme="neon" 
  size={64} 
/>
```

**Key points**:
- Uses Svelte 5 runes (`$props`, `$derived`)
- Reactive to prop changes
- Inline styles on wrapper span

### Web Component (Framework Agnostic)

```html
<script type="module">
  import 'seedface/webcomponent'; // auto-registers <seedface-avatar>
</script>

<seedface-avatar 
  value="user@example.com" 
  style="shape" 
  theme="forest" 
  variant="dark" 
  size="64"
></seedface-avatar>
```

```js
// Custom tag name
import { registerSeedface } from 'seedface/webcomponent';
registerSeedface('my-avatar');
```

**Key points**:
- Parses HTML attributes (kebab-case)
- Renders in open Shadow DOM
- Skips rendering without `value` attribute
- Boolean attributes: `border`, `shadow`, `animated` accept any truthy value except `'false'`, `'0'`, or empty string

## CLI Usage

```bash
# Generate SVG to file
seedface generate --value "jane@doe.com" --style shape --theme sunset --size 128 --out avatar.svg

# Generate SVG to stdout
seedface generate --value "jane@doe.com"

# Generate PNG (requires @resvg/resvg-js)
seedface generate --value "jane@doe.com" --out avatar.png --size 256
```

**All CLI options**:
- `--value` (required): seed string
- `--out`: output file path (.svg or .png)
- `--size`: px size (default 64)
- `--style`: character | shape | gradient | rings | pixel
- `--theme`: theme ID from `listThemes()`
- `--variant`: light | dark
- `--radius`: full | none | <number>
- `--displayValue`: override initials
- `--background`: bg color override
- `--color`: fg color override
- `--border`: add border
- `--borderSize`: border width
- `--borderColor`: border color
- `--shadow`: add drop shadow
- `--animated`: add CSS animation
- `--salt`: extra entropy
- `--png`: force PNG output
- `--help`: show help

PNG output requires optional `@resvg/resvg-js` dependency.

## Critical Implementation Details

### Determinism Guarantees

1. **Hash function**: `cyrb53` for string → number conversion
2. **PRNG**: `mulberry32` seeded from hash + optional salt
3. **No external state**: No timestamps, no `Math.random()`, no network calls
4. **Cache**: Results memoized by full option set for performance

### Initials Extraction Logic

```ts
function getInitials(value: string, displayValue?: string): string {
  if (displayValue) return displayValue.slice(0, 2).toUpperCase();
  
  const words = value.split(/[\s._@\-+]+/).filter(Boolean);
  if (words.length >= 2) return (words[0][0] + words[1][0]).toUpperCase();
  
  const letters = value.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9]/g, '');
  if (letters.length >= 2) return letters.slice(0, 2).toUpperCase();
  
  return (letters[0] || '?').toUpperCase();
}
```

### XML Escaping

All user-controlled content in SVG must be escaped:
- `<` → `&lt;`
- `>` → `&gt;`
- `&` → `&amp;`
- `'` → `&apos;`
- `"` → `&quot;`

### Color Validation

Only 3 or 6 digit hexadecimal values accepted (with or without `#`):
- Valid: `#fff`, `#ffffff`, `fff`, `ffffff`, `#F00`, `f0a`
- Invalid: `red`, `rgb(255,0,0)`, `rgba(...)`, `hsl(...)`

### Size Constraints

- Minimum: 8px
- Maximum: 512px
- Values are rounded to nearest integer

### Radius Handling

- `'full'` → 50% (circle)
- `'none'` → 0% (square)
- Number → converted to percentage relative to size

### Animation Behavior

When `animated=true`:
- Load animation: scale from 0.82 to 1, fade in
- Hover effect: scale to 1.08, rotate 6deg
- Respects `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` media query

### Border Rendering

Border drawn inside the clipping path:
- Position: centered on edge
- Width: specified in px
- Color: defaults to semi-transparent based on variant

### Shadow Effect

Drop shadow filter:
- Offset: (0, 2)
- StdDeviation: 3
- Color: `#0b0b0f` with 0.18 opacity

## Change Guidelines for AI Agents

### Safe Changes ✅

1. Add new themes in `src/core/themes.ts`
2. Add new shapes in `src/core/shapes.ts` (append only, preserve order)
3. Add new public options (thread through all adapters + CLI)
4. Performance optimizations that preserve output
5. Documentation updates
6. Bug fixes that restore intended behavior

### Unsafe Changes ❌

1. Change existing theme palettes (breaks established avatars)
2. Reorder existing shapes (changes identities)
3. Modify public API contracts without version consideration
4. Add runtime dependencies to `src/core/`
5. Introduce non-deterministic behavior
6. Break SSR/RSC compatibility
7. Remove `prefers-reduced-motion` support
8. Change exports or module structure

### Validation Checklist

Before submitting changes:

```bash
npm run typecheck  # TypeScript validation
npm test           # Determinism and behavior tests
npm run build      # Build verification
```

## Public Entry Points

- `seedface`: Core exports (`generateAvatar`, themes, shapes, RNG, hashes, types)
- `seedface/react`: `Avatar`, `useAvatar`, React types
- `seedface/vue`: Vue `Avatar` component
- `seedface/svelte`: Svelte `Avatar` component  
- `seedface/webcomponent`: `SeedfaceAvatar`, `registerSeedface`
- `seedface/cli`: CLI binary (`seedface generate ...`)

## Source of Truth Files

When modifying Seedface, always reference these canonical sources:

1. **Types**: `src/core/types.ts` - definitive `AvatarOptions` and `AvatarData`
2. **Generation**: `src/core/generate.ts` - core logic, caching, SVG building
3. **Themes**: `src/core/themes.ts` - theme definitions and palette helpers
4. **Shapes**: `src/core/shapes.ts` - 69 shape path definitions
5. **Hash/RNG**: `src/core/hash.ts`, `src/core/prng.ts` - determinism foundation

## Common Integration Patterns

### Pattern 1: User List with Lazy Loading

```tsx
import { Avatar } from 'seedface/react';

function UserList({ users }) {
  return (
    <ul>
      {users.map(user => (
        <li key={user.id}>
          <Avatar value={user.email} size={32} />
          {user.name}
        </li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  );
}
```

### Pattern 2: Dynamic Theme Selection

```ts
import { generateAvatar, listThemes } from 'seedface';

const themes = listThemes();
const randomTheme = themes[Math.floor(Math.random() * themes.length)];

const avatar = generateAvatar({
  value: userId,
  theme: randomTheme.id
});
```

### Pattern 3: Custom Initials Override

```tsx
<Avatar 
  value="john.doe@example.com"
  displayValue="JD"  // Force specific initials
  style="character"
/>
```

### Pattern 4: Branded Colors

```tsx
<Avatar 
  value={user.id}
  background="#3B82F6"  // Brand blue
  color="#FFFFFF"       // White text
  style="gradient"
/>
```

### Pattern 5: OG Image Generation (Server-Side)

```ts
import { generateAvatar } from 'seedface';

export async function GET({ url }: { url: URL }) {
  const email = url.searchParams.get('email');
  const avatar = generateAvatar({ 
    value: email, 
    size: 1200, 
    style: 'shape' 
  });
  
  return new Response(avatar.svg, {
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'image/svg+xml' }
  });
}
```

### Pattern 6: Email Avatar (Static PNG)

```bash
# Pre-generate avatars for email campaigns
seedface generate \
  --value "customer@email.com" \
  --size 96 \
  --style shape \
  --theme ocean \
  --out ./avatars/customer.png
```

## Troubleshooting

### Issue: Avatars differ between server and client

**Cause**: Using `variant="auto"` without proper SSR handling.

**Solution**: Explicitly set `variant` on server, or accept that server renders `light` as fallback.

### Issue: PNG export fails

**Cause**: Missing optional `@resvg/resvg-js` dependency.

**Solution**: Install with `npm i -D @resvg/resvg-js`

### Issue: Custom colors not applying

**Cause**: Invalid hex format.

**Solution**: Use only 3 or 6 digit hex values (e.g., `#fff`, `#ffffff`, `f00`).

### Issue: Shapes look different after update

**Cause**: Shape order changed or palette modified.

**Solution**: Never reorder existing shapes or modify existing theme palettes. Only append new items.

## Performance Characteristics

- **Raw SVG generation**: ~100k+ avatars/second
- **Memory**: Minimal footprint, cache prevents recomputation
- **Bundle size**: Core is zero-dependency, tree-shakeable
- **SSR**: Zero client JS when using static markup
- **Caching**: Automatic memoization by full option set

## Accessibility

- SVG includes `role="img"` and `aria-label`
- Title defaults to "Avatar for {value}"
- Can be overridden with `title` option
- Animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion`
